System Requirements¶

This section captures the top-level system requirements for the LED Stripe Controller. All requirements are traceable to software specifications and architecture elements.

Requirement: Operating System REQ_SYS_OS
status: open
tags: system, platform
links incoming: ARCH_CONTEXT, ARCH_DEPLOY

The system shall run on a Linux-based operating system.
Requirement: Target Hardware Platform REQ_SYS_HW
status: open
tags: system, hardware
rationale: Raspberry Pi provides GPIO, SPI bus, and sufficient CPU power for the intended use-case at low cost.
links incoming: ARCH_HW_RPI, ARCH_CONTEXT, ARCH_DEPLOY

The system shall execute on a Raspberry Pi (generation 3 or newer).
Requirement: LED Stripe Communication Protocol REQ_SYS_SPI
status: open
tags: system, hardware, spi
links incoming: ARCH_HW_SPI, ARCH_HW_LED, SPEC_SW_SPI_DRIVER, SPEC_SW_SPI_CLK, SPEC_SW_FRAME

The LED stripe shall be controlled via the SPI bus exposed by the Raspberry Pi.
Requirement: Realtime Control REQ_SYS_RT
status: open
tags: system, realtime
links incoming: ARCH_LAYERS, SPEC_SW_RT_PRIO, SPEC_SW_CPP_RT, SPEC_SW_IPC

The system shall guarantee that SPI frame transmission jitter does not exceed 100 µs.
Requirement: High-Level Control Interface REQ_SYS_HL
status: open
tags: system, api
links incoming: ARCH_LAYERS, SPEC_SW_PY_API, SPEC_SW_IPC

The system shall expose a high-level API that allows an operator to program LED animations and colour patterns without knowledge of the SPI protocol.
Requirement: Safety Shutdown REQ_SYS_SAFE
status: open
tags: system, safety
links incoming: ARCH_SW_SHUTDOWN, SPEC_SW_SHUTDOWN

In the event of a software fault or OS signal, the system shall transition all LEDs to the off state within 500 ms.

ID

Status

open

open

open

open
